summary: Implemented the M2 district operations surface with a client-core district read-model slice, a 4,000-row synthetic pressure fixture, manual virtualization, filtering, sorting, selection handoff to the map and panel, and Playwright coverage with measured UI derivation/selection timing.

tests_run.result: Showed task client/test changes plus pre-existing lead-owned orchestration changes in project/goal-mode-state.json, project/model-routing-state.json, project/tasks/thread-registry.json, and project/messages/routes.
Initial attempt failed because package-local src test files were outside the root Vitest include; tests were moved under tests/** and scripts updated.
Initial attempt failed for the same package-local Vitest include issue; tests were moved under tests/** and scripts updated.
Vite production web build passed; 23 modules transformed.
Architecture boundaries passed, determinism hashes matched, root Vitest passed 18 files/85 tests, web Vitest passed 1 file/3 tests.
Initial Playwright run found sort buttons hidden from the accessibility tree by aria-hidden on the header; fixed before rerun.
Chromium Playwright passed 2 tests, including 4,000-row virtualization, filtering, sorting, selection handoff, and measured derivation/selection timing assertions.
Prettier check, ESLint, recursive typecheck, and architecture check passed.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M2-CLIENT-DISTRICT-LIST-001
tests_run.result: Parsed task and handoff JSON; verified task id/status/route and required handoff fields.

decisions: Used Playwright coverage instead of Storybook because apps/storybook does not exist in this checkout and root .storybook config is outside the task allowed paths.
Added manual fixed-row virtualization in @monsoon/ui rather than introducing a production dependency.
Kept the 4,000-row fixture explicitly synthetic with display names like Synthetic District 0001 and a provenance note stating it is not formal historical district content.
Kept UI logic on read-model fields only; no sim-core formulas or WorldState snapshots were copied into client storage.
risks: The district list currently uses a synthetic pressure read-model slice in the web shell; a later integration task should replace it with live worker query projection when the client worker query adapter is expanded.
Playwright still reports the pre-existing Vite dynamic import warning from apps/web/src/worker/hello-simulation-adapter.mjs; this task did not modify that adapter.
Known non-blocking M2 save/load state.m2 omission remains unchanged as required.
requested_action: Review M2 district list implementation, virtualization behavior, performance evidence, and boundary compliance.
